title: Supermarkets drive novel ‘direct marketing’ channels for fresh produce from small farms in Romania
description: A practice abstract describing how major supermarkets in Romania have established cooperative schemes to integrate small-scale producers into their supply chains, offering farmers fixed prices and stable incomes through direct marketing channels. It analyses the advisory support, control mechanisms, and power dynamics within these supermarket-driven cooperatives and highlights emerging arrangements aimed at balancing bargaining power and expanding services to participants.
